---
title: "Manuscripts"
description: "Research projects using jaxcmr"
---

This section showcases research manuscripts and projects that use jaxcmr for computational memory modeling. Each project is a self-contained Quarto manuscript with its own analysis notebooks and results.

## Active Projects

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [TalmiEEG](/projects/TalmiEEG/)

Investigating EEG correlates of memory encoding and retrieval using CMR models. Explores how neural signals relate to model parameters.

**Status**: In progress
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [RepFR](/projects/repfr/)

Repetition effects in free recall. Modeling how repeated items affect temporal context and retrieval dynamics.

**Status**: In progress
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-6}
### [CRU to CMR](/projects/cru_to_cmr/)

Comparing Context Retrieval and Updating (CRU) models with CMR. Systematic evaluation of model architectures and parameter recovery.

**Status**: In progress
:::

:::

## Completed Work

Additional manuscripts and analyses are available in the `work/` directory, including reproductions of classic memory studies:

- **HealeyKahana2014** - Large-scale free recall replication
- **LohnasKahana2014** - Variable list length effects
- **Murdock1962** - Classic serial position data

## Project Structure

Each manuscript project follows a consistent structure:

```
projects/project-name/
├── _quarto.yml          # Quarto configuration
├── index.qmd            # Main manuscript
├── references.bib       # Bibliography
├── notebooks/           # Analysis notebooks
├── results/             # Figures and tables
└── notes/               # Working notes
```

Projects render as subsites to `docs/projects/<name>/` and are linked from this page.

## Starting a New Project

To create a new manuscript project:

1. Copy an existing project structure
2. Update `_quarto.yml` with your project info
3. Set the output directory: `output-dir: ../../docs/projects/your-project`
4. Add your manuscript content to `index.qmd`
5. Render with `quarto render` from the project directory
